prose: Fukui reactivity indices for pyridine were computed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard', PySCF RKS backend, density-fit) in the gas phase, charge 0 / multiplicity 1 for the neutral species (cation: charge +1, mult 2; anion: charge −1, mult 2). Partial charges were obtained via the Mulliken scheme (PySCF). Geometry was taken from the user-supplied file /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz without further relaxation. All three SCF calculations (N, N−1, N+1 electrons) converged. Fukui sum checks passed: sum(f+) = 1.0000, sum(f−) = 1.0000. No engine warnings were reported. The headline value reported is the maximum f+ (electrophilic Fukui index) = 0.48422572 at atom 1 (N), identifying nitrogen as the most electrophilic site (most susceptible to nucleophilic attack). The most nucleophilic site (highest f−) is C4 (atom 4) with f− = 0.11826. Integrity verdict: trustworthy = true.
